criteria.name: examples_validate_with_wrappers
criteria.acceptance_criterion: scripts/validate-config.{ps1,sh} validates every *.yaml under config/examples (application, schedules, llm-providers, providers/cloudflare) and exits 0.
evidence: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\scripts\validate-config.ps1 exited 0 and printed OK for application.example.yaml (ApplicationConfig), llm-providers.example.yaml (LlmProvidersConfig), schedules.example.yaml (SchedulesConfig), and providers\cloudflare.example.yaml (ProviderConfig), ending: All 4 configuration file(s) valid.
Git Bash path C:\Program Files\Git\bin\bash.exe was available; bash scripts/validate-config.sh exited 0 and printed OK for all four example configs, ending: All 4 configuration file(s) valid.

criteria.name: typed_models_forbid_unknown_fields
criteria.acceptance_criterion: Each configuration family has a typed Pydantic model that forbids unknown fields; an unknown field produces an actionable error naming the file and the field path with a non-zero exit.
evidence: apps/api/app/config/models.py defines ApplicationConfig, SchedulesConfig, LlmProvidersConfig, and ProviderConfig; all inherit _Base with model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid").

Adversarial command .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m app.config.cli validate <TEMP>\unknown-extra-field.yaml exited 1 and printed: FAIL <file>; - application.typo_field: Extra inputs are not permitted (type=extra_forbidden); 1 of 1 configuration file(s) failed validation.

criteria.name: malformed_yaml_actionable_line_column
criteria.acceptance_criterion: Malformed YAML produces an actionable error naming the file with the line and column of the syntax error, with a non-zero exit.
evidence: Adversarial command .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m app.config.cli validate <TEMP>\malformed-yaml.yaml exited 1 and printed: FAIL <file>; - YAML syntax error at line 4 column 4: expected <block end>, but found '<block mapping start>'; 1 of 1 configuration file(s) failed validation.

criteria.name: missing_required_field_actionable
criteria.acceptance_criterion: A missing required field produces an actionable error naming the field path (type=missing), with a non-zero exit.
evidence: Adversarial command .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m app.config.cli validate <TEMP>\missing-required-field.yaml exited 1 and printed: FAIL <file>; - provider.name: Field required (type=missing); 1 of 1 configuration file(s) failed validation.

For schedules: .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m app.config.cli emit-schema schedules exited 0; ConvertFrom-Json parsed stdout; PARSED: type=object properties=True root_additionalProperties=False additionalProperties_false_count=4; stdout first line { and last line }.

For provider: .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m app.config.cli emit-schema provider exited 0; ConvertFrom-Json parsed stdout; PARSED: type=object properties=True root_additionalProperties=False additionalProperties_false_count=4; stdout first line { and last line }.

criteria.name: secret_safety
criteria.acceptance_criterion: Secret safety: a raw inline secret key (api_key, password, token, secret, etc.) anywhere in a file is rejected; secrets are referenced only via *_env environment-variable names; a non-env-name reference is rejected; the detect-secrets scan stays green and no secret value is printed.
evidence: Adversarial inline-secret command exited 1 and printed: FAIL <file>; - llm.providers.gemini.api_key: inline secret key 'api_key' is forbidden; reference an environment variable via 'api_key_env' instead (values live in the environment, never in the repository); 1 of 1 configuration file(s) failed validation.
Inline-secret test used sentinel value LEAK_SENTINEL_DO_NOT_PRINT_173; output check printed SECRET_LEAK_CHECK: passed (sentinel value was not echoed).

Adversarial bad-env-reference command exited 1 and printed: FAIL <file>; - llm.providers.gemini.api_key_env: String should match pattern '^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$' (type=string_pattern_mismatch); 1 of 1 configuration file(s) failed validation.
scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit output included: ==> Secret scan; PASS: Secret scan.

Secret grep of apps/api/app/config, tests/unit/test_config_system.py, docs/CONFIGURATION.md, and config/examples found only secret-key vocabulary, environment-variable names GEMINI_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and the synthetic negative-test literal sk-should-never-be-here; no real credential value was found in source, docs, tests, or examples.

criteria.name: offline_pytest_suite
criteria.acceptance_criterion: The offline pytest suite (valid examples, unknown/malformed/missing/bad-cron/threshold/mcp/inline-secret/env-name, schema export, CLI) passes.
evidence: pwsh -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\scripts\test.ps1 exited 0; pytest collected 67 items with 65 passed, 2 skipped in 2.30s; tests\unit\test_config_system.py .......................; tests\unit\test_requirements_sync.py ...; TESTS PASSED.
The two skips were integration stack tests gated by ATLAS_STACK_BASE_URL not being set, matching the prompt's expected no-live-stack condition.

end_to_end_steps_run: Confirmed the F003 slice-1 contract identity and required Level 2 evaluation gate.
Validated all four example YAML files through both PowerShell and Git Bash wrappers.
Ran the full repository test and check scripts requested by the contract.
Crafted six adversarial YAML files outside the repository and verified every required failure mode exits non-zero with file-scoped output.
Verified inline-secret rejection does not echo the sentinel secret value.
Emitted and parsed JSON Schema for all four families and confirmed additionalProperties=false.
Corroborated secret-scan, product-truth, and dependency hygiene constraints.
Confirmed agent-state/feature_list.json still has F003 passes:false and was not edited.
notes: Disposition is passed for F003 slice 1 only. The overall F003 epic remains incomplete because domain model/migrations and Z0 classification slices are out of scope and still pending.

A first schema-summary helper command had an evaluator-side quoting SyntaxError after the CLI itself exited 0; the schema commands were immediately rerun and parsed successfully with PowerShell ConvertFrom-Json. This did not affect the implementation under test.

Only repository files intentionally written by this evaluator are agent-state/evaluation.json (overwrite) and agent-state/progress.md (append). No commit, push, merge, or feature_list.json edit was performed.
recommendations: Treat slice 1 as evaluator-approved, but keep F003 passes:false until slices 2 and 3 and a full F003 Level 2 close-out evaluation pass.
Proceed next with the approved F003 slice 2 domain model/Alembic migration contract rather than changing the feature ledger to passed.
